title: cochlearia officinalis var. pyrenaica
intro: Cochlearia officinalis var. pyrenaica, also known as Pyrenean scurvy grass, is a variety of the sea-purslane plant that grows in the Pyrenees mountains. This plant is valued for its high vitamin C content and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cochlearia officinalis var. pyrenaica include reducing the risk of scurvy and other vitamin C deficiency-related diseases, as well as offering potential protection against certain types of cancer and cardiovascular disease. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and topical creams for various health applications.
